evidence_items.snippet: was en- hanced 5,500-fold relative to its aqueous solubility. Up to 50%, the pK a of ibuprofen shifted from 4.44 to 5.68. Infrared spectroscopy confirmed the presence of ibuprofen in both monomer and dimer forms in vehicles containing 75 and 100% ethanol. In silicone membranes, the flux of ibuprofen increased 8-fold over the range of ethanol concentrations studied (0–100%) relative to aqueous solutions, with the highest flux observed for 100% ethanol. However, in skin, the flux of ibuprofen was optimal for 50: 50

ation Skin Pharmacol Physiol 2009;22:15–21 17 ducted with membranes pretreated with ethanol for 24 h to fur- ther investigate the influence of ethanol on ibuprofen perme- ation. Flux values were calculated from the cumulative amount of drug diffusing into the receptor compartment as a function of time. The slope of this profile was determined once steady state had been reached. Values of the permeability coefficient k p , Kh and D/h 2 were calculated by fitting the data to equation 2: (2) 2 2 2 2 2 2 1 1 2 1 exp 6

pecac multiple reflection accessory with a zinc sel- enide crystal was used and data were acquired using OPUS soft- ware, version 5.5 (Bruker, Germany). Results and Discussion Solubility Studies Addition of 25% ethanol increased the solubility of ibuprofen to 343 mg/ml, a 3,800-fold enhancement rela- tive to the drug’s aqueous solubility ( table 1 ). In pure eth- anol, the solubility is 5,500-fold higher than in water. Ibu- profen has a reported pK a of 4.41 [11] , explaining the con- siderable solubility enhanceme

provenance.route_notes: The study provides extensive data on ibuprofen permeation through skin, indicating the influence of ethanol in formulations.

evidence_items.snippet: Steady-state flux of ibuprofen across human skin...
pH 7.4 phosphate-buffered saline as receptor fluid
100 μl of ibuprofen solution
Ethanol as cosolvent
Ibuprofen solubility enhancement in ethanol
Flux values through skin are shown in table 5
The experiments were conducted over 24 hours
Diffusion area of 1 cm²
Franz diffusion cells were used for permeation studies.

provenance.route_notes: The study provides extensive data on ibuprofen permeation through skin, indicating the influence of ethanol in formulations.
